授業目的: 本授業の目的は、スピーキングやライティングの訓練を通じて、CEFR-J A2.1 レベルに相当する具体的なトピックについて会話をしたり、発表したり、書いたりする力を身につけることである。
到達目標: ・簡単な英語で意見や気持ちをやりとりしたり、賛成や反対などの自分の意見を伝えたり、物や人を比較したりすることができる。・一連の簡単な語句や文を使って、意見や計画を理由を挙げて短く述べることができる。・身の回りの出来事や趣味、場所、仕事などについて、個人的経験や自分に直接必要のある領域であれば、簡単な描写を書くことができる。・基礎的な日常生活語彙や表現を用いて、感想や意見などを短く書くことができる。
授業方法: This course adopts a communicative and learner-centred approach. Students will engage in interactive lectures, peer discussions, and group work that encourage meaningful language use and critical reflection. Learning is supported through a variety of assessment tasks, including vocabulary quizzes, listening activities, and individual and group assignments, allowing students to demonstrate their developing skills in multiple ways.本授業は、コミュニカティブかつ学習者中心のアプローチを採用しています。学生は、双方向型の講義、学生同士のディスカッション、そして意味のある言語使用と批判的な省察を促すグループ活動に取り組みます。学習は、語彙クイズ、リスニング活動、個人およびグループ課題など、多様な評価課題を通して支えられ、学生はさまざまな形で自身の言語運用能力の伸長を示すことができます。

教科書/Required texts1: Ryoya Kobayashi, Ayed Hasian, Seishirou Ibaraki, Yoko Nanako, Lidija Elliott
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading1: Vocab Quizzes (4) (20%); Individual Speaking Task (20%); Group Project Presentation (20%); Listening Task (2) (20%); Online Activity (2) (20%)
成績評価Grading2: 備考　Note (1)    ATTENDANCE① If students miss four classes, they will fail the course.② Handout the document “Department Regulations Regarding Students Being Absent or Late” during the first class of the semester and request that students follow the regulations. (2)    In-Class ExamsExams will not be given during the designated exam period. Instead, final exams will be given in the second to last week of each semester, and students should be given feedback on their test performance during the last class.(3)    Normal chapter tests and smaller quizzes (such as 10 point quizzes occurring multiple times during a semester and totaling 30% of the final grade) are to be handled at the discretion of the instructor. In the event that students miss mid-terms, final exams, or final presentations that are scheduled during class time, please allow for one time of make-up opportunities according to the general rules listed below. Please consider limiting their full score to 80% credit for these make-up or supplementary tests, in general. However, in the cases described in “Regarding Make-up Tests and Presentations for Absent Students”, please allow for full credit.(4)    Plagiarism▪    Students must complete the writing assignments, such as composition and reports, with their own abilities.▪    Students must never copy from other students’ assignments, descriptions found in the Internet and other similar sources.▪    Students must never have their friends borrow their assignments and reports, least such actions should develop plagiarism.▪    The students who were involved in any plagiarism, not only the student who copied other student’s assignment and report but also the student who showed his / her assignment and report, can be subject to punishment.（5）Change of PlansPlans of individual lessons and studies required outside classes are subject to change.

name: 【100】英語表現Ｂ　１０／English Writing B
担当者: トッド　アレン　Ｊ．（Todd Allen James）
成績評価備考:
備考　Note

(1)    ATTENDANCE
① If students miss four classes, they will fail the course.
② Handout the document “Department Regulations Regarding Students Being Absent or Late” during the first class of the semester and request that students follow the regulations.
 

(2)    In-Class Exams
Exams will not be given during the designated exam period. Instead, final exams will be given in the second to last week of each semester, and students should be given feedback on their test performance during the last class.

(3)    Normal chapter tests and smaller quizzes (such as 10 point quizzes occurring multiple times during a semester and totaling 30% of the final grade) are to be handled at the discretion of the instructor. In the event that students miss mid-terms, final exams, or final presentations that are scheduled during class time, please allow for one time of make-up opportunities according to the general rules listed below. Please consider limiting their full score to 80% credit for these make-up or supplementary tests, in general. However, in the cases described in “Regarding Make-up Tests and Presentations for Absent Students”, please allow for full credit.

(4)    Plagiarism
▪    Students must complete the writing assignments, such as composition and reports, with their own abilities.
▪    Students must never copy from other students’ assignments, descriptions found in the Internet and other similar sources.
▪    Students must never have their friends borrow their assignments and reports, least such actions should develop plagiarism.
▪    The students who were involved in any plagiarism, not only the student who copied other student’s assignment and report but also the student who showed his / her assignment and report, can be subject to punishment.

（5）Change of Plans
Plans of individual lessons and studies required outside classes are subject to change.
